display.purpose: One plain-language publishing decision per screen.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Selected claims, buttons, and segmented controls.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single strongest action in a workflow region.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: A quiet action that stays subordinate to the publishing decision.

components.name: Evidence Status Chip
components.description: A written result paired with semantic color.

components.description: A flat work surface with a hairline border.
components.html:
What Saga found

Validated evidence conflicts with this claim.

components.name: Text Audit Field
components.description: A large, calm field for the document being checked.
components.html: <textarea class="ds-text-area" placeholder="Paste an AI-written report, article, or draft."></textarea>

components.name: Claim Highlight
components.description: An inline, keyboard-operable claim selection inside the original document.

narrative.overview: Saga should feel like a clear document review on a bright laptop during a time-limited publishing decision. The product is forensic without looking hostile, technical without leading with implementation details, and confident without using spectacle. The eye should move from outcome, to claims that need attention, to corrected prose, and only then to deeper proof. This system explicitly rejects dense internal diagnostics consoles, terminal cosplay and generic black-and-neon AI dashboards, and decorative AI effects. It uses quiet paper surfaces, strong editorial type hierarchy, compact written statuses, and progressive disclosure to keep attention on the decision.
keyCharacteristics: Light neutral canvas with crisp white work surfaces
Forest ink for authority and a restrained evidence green
Large plain-language outcomes with compact supporting counts
Flat structure, hairline dividers, and limited ambient shadow
Short state transitions used only to explain interaction

rules.name: The Evidence Color Rule
rules.body: Semantic color always appears with a written status. Color never carries meaning alone.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Green signals evidence or success. It is not decorative branding scattered across the screen.

rules.name: The Answer First Rule
rules.body: The largest type states what the user should do, not the internal system name.

rules.name: The Flat Review Rule
rules.body: Content and evidence stay flat at rest. Shadow never replaces hierarchy.

dos: Do lead with one plain-language audit outcome and no more than three supporting numbers.
Do keep evidence and technical provenance one deliberate action away.
Do use a minimum 44px target for primary actions and navigation.
Do respect reduced motion and keep state transitions between 180ms and 240ms.
Do preserve a visible distinction between fixed Demo results and Live web research.
donts: Don't build dense internal diagnostics consoles.
Don't use terminal cosplay and generic black-and-neon AI dashboards.
Don't use unexplained internal terms such as "Trust Passport" or "Flight Recorder" as primary navigation.
Don't use decorative glass, glow, and fake thinking animations.
Don't create repetitive grids of equal-weight cards.
Don't let technical metrics hide the publishing decision.
